title: Bulk report - Week 26
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market experienced an eventful week despite starting off quietly with the BCI 5TC dropping by $409 to $25,650 on Monday. However, the market turned around midweek with a surge in activity, especially in the Atlantic. Demand from South Brazil and West Africa to the Far East for July loaders drove a tightening of the ballaster list, significantly boosting rates resulting in the BCI 5TC increasing by $1,288 to $26,082.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market experienced an eventful week despite starting off quietly with the BCI 5TC dropping by $409 to $25,650 on Monday. However, the market turned around midweek with a surge in activity, especially in the Atlantic.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A further eroding of rates across the board for the Panamax market this week. The Atlantic returned smaller losses and throughout the week sources spoke of a two-tiered market between the mineral and grain trades, with the former seeing again hugely discounted rates, whilst $15,500 was agreed on a scrubber fitted vessel for a trans-Atlantic trip via NC South America.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: Mixed fortunes again for the sector, as ‘summertime blues' probably described the Atlantic whilst from Asia the week started on a positive note, although as it finished even here positive sentiment eroded. The Atlantic was rather positional, with demand from the US Gulf seemingly ebbing away, although a 63,000-dwt was heard to have fixed a fronthaul from NC South America at $26,000.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: A lackluster feel was seen across the handy sector as the week progressed. The Continent and Mediterranean were the highlights of the Atlantic, with a 38,000-dwt fixing from the Black Sea to Morocco with grains at $14,000 whilst a 33,000-dwt fixed from Middlesbrough to the US Gulf with a cargo of steels at $12,500.
